seo.title: About Able Care — Science-Led, Impact-Driven | Able Care
seo.description: Able Care is the falls prevention platform commercialized out of Imperial College London neuroscience research. FDA listed. CE marked. ISO 27001 certified.

blocks.backgroundImageAlt: Patient giving thumbs up during functional health assessment
blocks.headline: Science-led. Impact-driven.
blocks.subtitle: Able Care is the falls prevention platform for operators and clinicians who need objective, standardized falls risk screening across every site. Commercialized out of Imperial College London neuroscience research.

blocks.heading: From Imperial College to the frontline
blocks.body: Able Care was born from neuroscience research at Imperial College London. The founding team saw that functional health measurement was stuck in the analogue era: subjective, inconsistent, and disconnected from care workflows. They built a digital platform that captures four clinically validated metrics in under five minutes, usable by non-clinical staff, defensible to payers and regulators, and scalable across multi-site operations.
blocks.imageAlt: Imperial College London campus where Able Care research originated

blocks.heading: The problem we solve
blocks.body: Falls are the leading cause of injury-related death in adults over 65. Paper-based assessments are slow, subjective, and rarely standardized across sites. Fall detection technology looks at falls after they happen. Able Care sits upstream, screening every resident or client objectively and handing operators a standardized workflow they can roll out everywhere.
blocks.imageAlt: Diagram showing Able Care's upstream prevention approach

entries.body: Our two founders, Dr. Mike Mace and Dr. Paul Rinne, meet at Imperial College in London while completing their PhDs. They connect over a shared frustration: that functional health technology was stuck in the analog era — subjective, inconsistent, and disconnected from care workflows. They work together to begin designing the first iterations of a better technology for rehabilitation.

The first iteration of their idea goes to market. GripAble is a handheld sensor that measures grip strength and wrist range of motion. It connects to an app that allows patients to carry out upper limb neurorehabilitation by playing gamified activities.
GripAble is spun out of Imperial College London.
GripAble becomes the most widely distributed digital rehabilitation in the UK.
GripAble begins its expansion into the United States.

The team sets up a new brand — Able Care — to roll out new digital functional health products. It introduces Able Assess, a product that utilizes the same GripAble sensor to measure fall risk of individuals over 65.

members.name: Dr Paul Rinne
members.bio: Healthcare entrepreneur dedicated to creating digital health solutions focused on healthspan and longevity. Founder and CEO of GripAble and Able Care, committed to delivering value-based care through data-driven digital transformation.

members.name: Dr Mike Mace
members.credentials: PhD in Bioengineering, Imperial College London
members.bio: Former Research Associate in rehabilitation technology at Imperial College London's Human Robotics Group, where he led rehabilitation technology projects. Co-pioneered the GripAble device through collaboration with therapists, patients, and academic institutions.

members.name: Danielle Richards
members.bio: Commercial Director at both GripAble and Able Care, leading business and marketing strategy. Former Risk Analyst at Google. Passionate about the technology-health intersection with a proven track record scaling startups and scale-ups.

blocks.heading: See Able Assess in your workflow.
blocks.body: 20 minutes with a clinician who has run it in the field.
